sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Jim Cramer on Merck & Co.: “If We’re Really Going Into an Oil Shock-Induced Slowdown, I Think This One’s Likely an Outperformer””
feed.summary: Jim Cramer is bullish on Merck & Co. (NYSE: MRK), citing its strong performance driven by Keytruda and positive money flow. He views the stock as an outperformer, especially in an "oil shock-induced slowdown," and observes that recent pullbacks have been ideal entry points. Merck recently acquired a clinical-stage cancer drug company for $6.7 billion, a move that was well-received by the market.

feed.title: Gas, diesel price 'sticker shock' top of mind for consumers, Tractor Supply Company CEO says
feed.summary: Tractor Supply Company CEO Hal Lawton states that the "sticker shock" of gas and diesel prices is a primary concern for consumers. He also discussed the impact of fuel costs during an appearance on 'Varney & Co.', linking it to President Donald Trump's White House meeting with farmers and ranchers. The video clip highlights the economic implications of current fuel prices.

feed.title: Vanguard disaggregates holdings; Westinghouse shows 0 shares after realignment (WAB)
feed.summary: Vanguard Group has filed an amendment (Schedule 13G/A) for Westinghouse Air Brake Technologies Corp (WAB), reporting 0 shares owned and a 0% stake. This change is due to an internal realignment effective January 12, 2026, where certain Vanguard subsidiaries will now report beneficial ownership separately. The filing clarifies that this disaggregation is for reporting purposes under SEC Release No. 34-39538 and does not represent an economic sale of shares.

feed.title: Essex Property Trust (NYSE:ESS) Sets New 52-Week Low - Time to Sell?
feed.summary: Essex Property Trust (ESS) recently hit a new 52-week low, trading at $239.24. Analysts largely hold a "Hold" rating for the stock, with a consensus target price of $282.00, despite the company slightly missing quarterly EPS estimates but raising its dividend.

feed.title: Fortinet Stock Falls As Anthropic AI Leak Pressures Cybersecurity Stocks
feed.summary: Fortinet (NASDAQ: FTNT) stock fell after an alleged Anthropic AI leak raised concerns about AI's impact on cybersecurity, leading to sector-wide pressure. Although Anthropic clarified the incident was human error unrelated to AI tools, investors fear AI-native solutions could disrupt traditional security markets, putting valuation pressure on established companies like Fortinet.

feed.title: What Trump said about Caterpillar, other top agriculture brand costs
feed.summary: Former President Donald Trump announced that he wants major agriculture brands like John Deere, Case, and Caterpillar to reduce the costs of their tractors and equipment for farmers. Following his statement, Deere & Co shares dropped by 2%, and Case manufacturer CNH saw a 1% decline, while Caterpillar remained largely unchanged. Trump also outlined new loan guarantees for farmers and food suppliers through the U.S. Small Business Administration, updated renewable fuel standards, and stated he would seek further congressional relief for the agricultural sector.

feed.title: Raymond James Financial Has Lowered Expectations for BRP (TSE:DOO) Stock Price
feed.summary: Raymond James Financial has decreased its price target for BRP (TSE:DOO) from C$117 to C$115, suggesting a 24.7% upside. Despite this, several other analysts recently raised their targets, leading to a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating with an average price target of C$116.29. BRP shares were trading down 0.9% at C$92.20, with a market cap of C$6.74 billion and a P/E ratio of 19.87, and reported C$2.21 EPS on C$2.46 billion in revenue for the quarter.

feed.title: Donaldson (NYSE: DCI) president sells 20,600 shares after option exercise
feed.summary: Donaldson Co. president Guillermo Briseño completed an exercise-and-sell transaction involving 20,600 shares of common stock. On March 26, 2026, he exercised fully vested employee stock options across two tranches and subsequently sold all 20,600 shares at $84.97 per share. Following these transactions, Briseño directly holds 29,267 shares of Donaldson common stock.

feed.title: Kroger Investing In Ohio Stores
feed.summary: Kroger is investing $112 million in Ohio to build new stores and remodel existing ones. This expenditure aims to enhance the grocer's presence, support communities, and provide modern shopping experiences in central and northwest Ohio. The plan includes two new Kroger Marketplace locations opening this fall and renovations at 15 other stores.

feed.title: Internet Giants ETF Cuts Meta and Alphabet in Rebalance
feed.summary: The ALPS O’Shares Global Internet Giants ETF (OGIG) significantly reduced its exposure to Meta Platforms and Alphabet during its March rebalancing, capping both positions at 6%. This strategic shift aims to de-emphasize mega-cap stocks and capitalize on more attractive valuations in enterprise software infrastructure and undervalued internet platforms, aligning with Morningstar's 2026 Global Outlook. The fund added companies like Synopsys and Constellation Software while removing nine other holdings, and increased its weight in Oracle and Tencent, among others.

feed.title: Raymond James: Credit Union Program Partnership With Addition Financial
feed.summary: Raymond James Financial's Financial Institutions Division has partnered with Addition Financial Credit Union to support its investment program, Addition Financial Wealth Management. This new alignment will provide comprehensive wealth management services to Addition Financial's clients through Raymond James Financial Services. The partnership, led by financial advisor Jay Williams, aims to expand investment capabilities and offer digital tools to better serve credit union members with their financial planning needs.

feed.title: 51Talk full year 2025 financial and operational results
feed.summary: 51Talk Online Education Group (NYSE:COE) announced strong financial results for the full year ended December 31, 2025, with net revenues surging by 88.6% to $95.6 million, driven by its international business expansion. Active students increased by 79.3%, reaching approximately 170.3 thousand, reflecting robust demand in Southeast Asia and other emerging markets. While the company achieved a healthy operating cash inflow, increased sales and marketing expenses and investments in its AI platform led to widened operating and net losses for the year.

feed.title: Kroger to reportedly open two stores, invest $112 million in Ohio
feed.summary: Kroger plans to invest $112 million in Ohio to open two new stores and renovate existing ones, as part of a larger strategy to accelerate capital investment and expand its competitive position. The investment includes new Kroger Marketplace locations and enhanced shopping experiences in central and northwest Ohio, with two new stores costing approximately $30 million each. This expansion aligns with Kroger's aim to become a national retailer and increase new store builds by 30% in 2026.

feed.title: Fastenal stock consolidates as team gears up for Martinsville race
feed.summary: Fastenal (FAST) stock is consolidating near recent highs, trading between $44.70 and $45.30, exhibiting a balance of bullish and bearish momentum. The company announced its sponsorship for Ryan Preece and the No. 60 Body Guard Ford team at Martinsville. Technical indicators suggest short-to-medium-term selling pressure, but the long-term trend remains strong, with a high probability of price increase in the coming week.

feed.title: Vanguard disaggregates holdings; Waters Corp shows 0% stake (WAT)
feed.summary: Vanguard Group has filed an Amendment No. 16 to Schedule 13G/A, reporting 0% beneficial ownership and 0 shares in Waters Corp (WAT). This change is attributed to an internal realignment on January 12, 2026, where certain Vanguard subsidiaries will now report their holdings separately, referencing SEC Release No. 34-39538. The filing indicates a disaggregation of reporting rather than a sale of shares.

feed.title: Multimillion-dollar paycheck: Here's how much Norwegian's new CEO could make
feed.summary: Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings' new CEO, John Chidsey, is set to receive a multimillion-dollar compensation package. His pay is tied to the company's long-term performance, aligning with ongoing governance changes at the Miami-based cruise operator.

feed.title: Consumers Energy making upgrades to electric grid
feed.summary: Consumers Energy's Reliability Action Plan has been approved after a year-long process, allowing for significant upgrades to Michigan's electric grid. This investment aims to provide more reliable power to approximately 2 million homes and businesses, particularly during severe weather, and follows recent efforts to recover from an ice storm. The company stated that these upgrades have already reduced average outage times and will continue to improve grid resilience while helping customers manage their energy bills.

feed.title: Trump wants Deere, Irving-based Caterpillar to cut farmers’ tractor costs
feed.summary: Former President Donald Trump has called on major agricultural supply companies, including John Deere, Case (CNH), and Irving-based Caterpillar, to reduce the costs of tractors and equipment for farmers. Following his statement, shares of Deere & Co fell by 2% and CNH by 1%, while Caterpillar remained largely unchanged. Trump also announced measures to support agricultural communities, such as new loan guarantees from the SBA for farmers and food suppliers, and an update to renewable fuel standards.

feed.title: Vanguard disaggregates holdings; reports 0% stake in Zoetis (ZTS)
feed.summary: The Vanguard Group has filed an amended Schedule 13G/A for Zoetis Inc. (ZTS), reporting a 0% beneficial ownership stake. This change is due to an internal realignment effective January 12, 2026, where certain Vanguard subsidiaries will now report their holdings separately, as per SEC Release No. 34-39538. The filing indicates that The Vanguard Group itself no longer holds beneficial ownership of Zoetis shares, with future reporting expected from its disaggregated subsidiaries.

feed.title: Verizon Communications Inc. Financial Statements – BSESOF:BAC
feed.summary: The article provides financial statement information for Verizon Communications Inc. (BSESOF:BAC) on TradingView. It notes that there is currently no market capitalization, revenue, or net income data available for the company. Additionally, the company has never paid dividends and has no current plans to do so.

feed.title: Colgate-Palmolive: One of Corporate America’s Last Remaining DEI Holdouts
feed.summary: The National Legal and Policy Center (NLPC) is calling out Colgate-Palmolive as one of the few remaining corporations that has not rolled back its Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DEI) board mandates. NLPC has sponsored Proposal 4 for Colgate's May 8, 2026 Annual Meeting, urging the company to eliminate identity-based criteria for selecting directors, citing legal risks and deteriorating financial performance under its current board. The article highlights that many other major corporations, some after negotiations with NLPC, have already removed discriminatory language from their board selection processes.

feed.title: Carnival Corp Beats Q1 Expectations, Unveils $2.5B Buyback — So Why Is CCL Stock Falling Today?
feed.summary: Carnival Corp (CCL) exceeded Q1 revenue and earnings estimates and announced a new $2.5 billion share buyback program. Despite these positive indicators, the stock fell nearly 4% due to a reduced full-year 2026 profit outlook. This cut in guidance was attributed to volatility in fuel prices, specifically the surge in Brent crude futures following disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz.

feed.title: Jim Cramer on Paychex: “This is a stock that strangely has been crushed by AI disruption worries, I think they’re overblown”
feed.summary: Jim Cramer discussed Paychex, stating that the stock has been unduly impacted by fears of AI disruption. He believes these concerns are exaggerated and that the market has unfairly punished Paychex. This suggests Cramer sees potential value in the company despite the prevailing anxiety about AI's influence.

feed.title: Oracle’s Credit Risk Measure Nears Record High on AI-Debt Fears
feed.summary: Oracle's credit risk measure is approaching an all-time closing high, signaling investor concern over the company's substantial debt. This rise is attributed to worries about AI-related debt, alongside increasing oil prices and declining stock prices. The cost of protecting Oracle's debt against default has surged to 198.58 basis points, nearing its previous peak from December 2008.

feed.title: Jim Cramer breaks down the data center growth story for Generac Holdings
feed.summary: Jim Cramer highlighted Generac Holdings' potential for growth, specifically linking it to the expanding data center industry. He suggests that the company's products are becoming increasingly essential as data centers proliferate and require more robust power solutions. This connection to the critical infrastructure of the digital economy positions Generac for significant future expansion.

feed.title: Vanguard reports zero holdings in Wynn Resorts (WYNN) after Jan 12 realignment

The filing is administrative, reflecting a change in reporting structure rather than a sale of shares, and future filings from disaggregated Vanguard entities may show redistributed positions.

feed.title: Vanguard (WEC) amendment shows 0% stake after Jan 12, 2026 realignment

feed.title: Palantir Technologies Inc Stock (PLTR) Moved Down by 3.13% on Mar 27: Key Drivers Unveiled
feed.summary: Palantir Technologies Inc. (PLTR) stock dropped 3.13% on March 27, primarily influenced by broader market retreat among high-multiple technology stocks, concerns over its elevated valuation, and significant insider selling. Despite positive developments like its Maven Smart System designation, negative sentiment and regulatory scrutiny regarding a UK contract outweighed these factors. Technical indicators show a buy signal from MACD, neutral RSI, and oversold Williams %R, while media coverage remains low.

feed.title: Booking Holdings Inc Stock (BKNG) Moved Down by 3.18% on Mar 27: A Full Analysis
feed.summary: Booking Holdings Inc. (BKNG) stock declined by 3.18% on March 27, influenced by geopolitical uncertainty, a weakening broader market, and company-specific concerns regarding AI disruption and capital allocation. Despite some positive indicators like strong Q4 2025 earnings and an announced stock split, mixed analyst sentiment and bearish technical signals contributed to the negative movement. The company, a leader in the Cyclical Consumer Services sector, faces ongoing structural risks from AI advancements in the online travel agency business model.

feed.title: Borr Drilling, Comstock Resources, Green Plains, Core Natural Resources, and Clean Energy Fuels Stocks Trade Up, What You Need To Know
feed.summary: Several oil and gas stocks, including Borr Drilling, Comstock Resources, Green Plains, Core Natural Resources, and Clean Energy Fuels, saw increases in their share prices following a rally in the Oil & Gas sector. This surge is attributed to escalating geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, leading to fears of supply disruptions and rising crude oil prices, despite a broader market decline. Clean Energy Fuels, specifically, experienced an 8.1% jump, though its shares remain volatile and below its 52-week high.

feed.title: Vanguard disaggregates holdings; Zimmer Biomet (ZBH) shows 0 shares

This change is due to an internal realignment effective January 12, 2026, allowing certain Vanguard subsidiaries to report their holdings separately, as per SEC Release No. 34-39538, rather than indicating a sale of shares. The filing is administrative, clarifying reporting procedures and beneficial ownership attribution following the realignment.

feed.title: Eversource Takes a Hit as Regulators Reset ROE and Trigger Refund Risk
feed.summary: Eversource Energy (ES) is facing a significant hit as a new FERC ruling resets the New England Transmission Owners' base Return on Equity (ROE) to 9.57%, leading to a 100 basis point drag in 2026 and potential refund obligations. Despite this headwind and a recent stock pullback of 11.51%, Bank of America maintains a "Buy" rating with a $73 price target, citing Eversource's substantial capital plan of $26.5 billion through 2030, projected rate base growth, and a multi-decade dividend growth streak (currently yielding 4.49%). The company's pivot to a pure-play regulated utility status and long-term EPS growth guidance of 5% to 7% are expected to support its long-term investment appeal for retirement investors.

feed.title: Caterpillar (NYSE: CAT) CEO adds phantom stock units in deferred plan
feed.summary: Caterpillar CEO Joseph E. Creed has acquired 3 phantom stock units as part of a non-qualified deferred compensation plan on March 26, 2026. These units are economically equivalent to common stock but will be settled in cash upon his retirement or separation from service. Following this transaction, Creed now holds a total of 11,583 phantom stock units, with two new units credited at $703.19 each and one contributed without consideration.

feed.title: Vanguard (GWW) amendment shows 0% ownership after Jan 12, 2026 realignment

The filing, signed on March 27, 2026, indicates that Vanguard no longer directly holds or is deemed to hold these securities.

feed.title: Steel Dynamics Announces Retirement of Two Long-Tenured Directors
feed.summary: Steel Dynamics (STLD) announced that co-founder Richard Teets, Jr. and director Gabriel Shaheen will retire from the board after the May 6, 2026 annual meeting, reducing the board size from nine to seven members. The company emphasized that their departures are amicable and not due to any disagreements. Teets was highlighted as a key architect of the company's growth, while Shaheen was praised for his financial acumen.

feed.title: Vanguard (NYSE: WST) reports 0% stake after January 12, 2026 realignment
feed.summary: The Vanguard Group has filed an amendment to its Schedule 13G/A, reporting 0% beneficial ownership of West Pharmaceutical Services Inc (WST) common stock. This change follows an internal realignment on January 12, 2026, where certain Vanguard subsidiaries will now report their holdings separately. This disaggregation means Vanguard Group no longer beneficially owns these shares, but individual Vanguard subsidiaries may continue to hold WST stock.

feed.title: Vanguard (ZBRA) reports 0 shares of Zebra after internal realignment
